evals.prompt: Create a Riverpod provider that fetches a list of products from a repository and exposes loading, data, and error states to the UI.
evals.expected_output: @riverpod annotation on AsyncNotifier<List<Product>>, build() calls repository.getProducts(), UI uses ref.watch(productsProvider) with .when(data:, loading:, error:)

evals.prompt: I need a provider that watches the cart count and triggers a navigation side effect when the cart is emptied. How do I do this in Riverpod?
evals.expected_output: ref.listen(cartProvider, (prev, next) { if (next.isEmpty) context.go('/empty-cart'); }) in ConsumerWidget build() or ConsumerStatefulWidget — not inside provider itself

evals.prompt: How do I override a Riverpod provider in widget tests to return a mock repository?
evals.expected_output: ProviderScope(overrides: [repositoryProvider.overrideWithValue(MockRepository())], child: MyWidget()) in pumpWidget, use riverpod_lint to catch missing overrides

should_not_trigger: How do I create a BLoC for the cart feature?
Set up GetX state management with observables
Add a loading spinner widget to the product list screen
Write a unit test for the repository layer
